title: ficaria verna var. grandiflora
intro: Ficaria verna var. grandiflora, also known as the large-flowered meadow crane's-bill or greater celandine, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a purgative and emetic, due to its high content of protoanemonin, a toxic compound that can cause symptoms like nausea and vomiting when ingested. The plant's health benefits include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Ficaria verna var. grandiflora is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves, and can be dried or made into a poultice to apply topically to affected areas.
